back_translation: Five days later, the high priest Ananias came down together with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus. These men filed a formal accusation against Paul before the governor.

When Paul had been called in, Tertullus began to make his accusation, saying, "Most excellent Felix, we have enjoyed lasting peace because of you, and various reforms have come about in this nation through your prudent administration."
We acknowledge this in every way and in every place, most excellent Felix, with all gratitude.
But so as not to take up more of your time, I beseech you to hear us briefly with your customary clemency.
We have found this man to be a public menace, one who stirs up disturbances among the Jews throughout the world, and a leader of the sect of the Nazarenes.
He even attempted to defile the temple, so we seized him.
When you yourself examine this man, you will be able to know the truth concerning all the charges that we have brought against him."
The Jewish side joined in the attack as well, claiming that these statements were true.
When the governor nodded for Paul to speak, he answered, "I know that you have been a judge over this nation for many years, so I gladly make my defense concerning myself.
You can verify that it has been only twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem to worship.
And they did not find me debating with anyone in the temple, or inciting a crowd, neither in the synagogues nor anywhere in the city.
Nor are they able to prove to you the charges they are now bringing against me.

But this I confess to you: that according to the Way (which they call a sect), I worship the God of our fathers, believing all things that are in accordance with the Law and that are written in the books of the Prophets,
having a hope in God — which these men themselves also accept — that there will be a resurrection from the dead, both of the righteous and of the unrighteous.
Because of this, I myself strive to keep a conscience that is free from blame before God and before fellow human beings at all times.
Many years later, I came back to my nation in order to bring alms and to present offerings.
While I was presenting those offerings, they found me in the temple, having purified myself, not with a crowd nor with any disturbance.
But there were some Jews from the province of Asia, and it is they who ought to have come to appear before you and bring charges, if they had any accusation against me.
Otherwise, let these men say for themselves what wrongdoing they found in me when I stood before the Sanhedrin council,
unless it was concerning this one statement that I shouted while I was standing in their midst, 'It is concerning the resurrection from the dead that I am being tried before you today.'"

But Felix, who knew the matters concerning the Way with considerable thoroughness, adjourned the hearing of their case, saying, "When Lysias the chiliarch comes down, I will render a verdict on your case."
He ordered the centurion to keep Paul in custody, but to allow him some liberty, and to permit his friends to attend to his needs.
Several days later, Felix returned together with Drusilla, his wife, who was a Jewish woman. He gave orders to have Paul brought, and listened to him speak about faith in Jesus Christ.

While Paul was reasoning about righteousness, self-control, and the judgment that is to come, Felix became frightened and answered, "Go away for now. When I have an opportunity, I will summon you back again."
At the same time, Felix was hoping that Paul would give him bribe money. Therefore he had Paul brought to him often and conversed with him.
When two years had passed, Felix received Porcius Festus as his successor in office. Felix wanted to please the Jews, so he left Paul in detention.
